name: Newtonian inverse-square law down to ~50 micrometers
requirement: Any candidate framework that introduces extra spatial dimensions or new long-range forces must respect laboratory tests of the gravitational inverse-square law at sub-millimeter distances. Yukawa-type deviations of the form V(r) = -G m1 m2/r [1 + alpha exp(-r/lambda)] are excluded for alpha ~ 1 down to lambda ~ 52 micrometers.

regime.description: Sub-millimeter to ~10 micrometer separations between rotating tungsten test masses; torsion-balance and microcantilever experiments.

current_bound.reference: Lee et al., 'New Test of the Gravitational 1/r^2 Law at Separations down to 52 micrometers', PRL 124, 101101 (2020), arXiv:2002.11761

what_it_excludes: Large-extra-dimension scenarios (ADD-style) with two extra dimensions are excluded down to compactification radii below ~30 micrometers, ruling out the original 'gravity becomes strong at the TeV scale via mm-sized extra dimensions' proposals. Forbids generic moduli or radion fields with gravitational-strength couplings and ranges above ~50 micrometers.

primary_references.citation: Lee et al. (Eot-Wash), 'New Test of the Gravitational 1/r^2 Law at Separations down to 52 micrometers', PRL 124, 101101 (2020)
Adelberger, Heckel, Nelson, 'Tests of the Gravitational Inverse-Square Law', Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 53, 77 (2003)
